Name: Lisa A English | DOB: 7/25/1964 | MRN: 055656 | PCP: Jung Wook Kang Smith, MD | Legal Name: Lisa A English Discharge Summary Signed Jun 25, 2026 Deaconess Care Group Discharge Summary Deaconess Hospital 6/23/2026 3:15 PM Patient Name: Lisa A English Date of Birth: 7/25/1964 MRN: 055656 CODE STATUS: Full Code Admit Date: 6/19/2026 Discharge Date: 6/23/2026 Date of Service: 6/23/2026 Admitting Physician: Joseph A Mansour, MD Attending Physician: Ahmad T Malik, MD Primary Care Physician: Kang Smith, Jung Wook Admitting Diagnosis: Morbid obesity due to excess calories (HCC) Physical debility DM hyperosmolarity type II, uncontrolled (HCC) Chronic respiratory failure with hypoxia and hypercapnia Generalized anxiety disorder Dependence on non-invasive ventilation COPD, end-stage Discharge Diagnoses: Morbid obesity due to excess calories (HCC) Physical debility DM hyperosmolarity type II, uncontrolled (HCC) Chronic respiratory failure with hypoxia and hypercapnia Generalized anxiety disorder Dependence on non-invasive ventilation COPD, end-stage B/L Subconjunctival hemorrhage Discharge Summary by Ahmad Malik at 06/23/26 1515

# Page 2^page=2
Chief complaint: Debility History The patient is a 61 y.o. female with a past medical history notable for chronic debility/wheelchair-bound, former smoker/severe COPD oxygen dependent on chronic steroids and dupixent, chronic respiratory failure with hypoxia and hypercapnia, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes mellitus, CAD, depression/anxiety/PTSD, chronic anemia, kidney stones, GERD. She was discharged home yesterday from hospital due to severe COPD exacerbation, short words Augmentin for possible right upper extremity cellulitis. She presented back from home via EMS due to an inability to care for herself, she requires help at home in transition between recliner to the scooter, her son is not able to help her, she has been sitting in the recliner since discharge yesterday from hospital. Afebrile, heart rate 180, respiratory 20, BP 152/69, 97% on 2 L Lab significant for WBC 11.5, hemoglobin 9.3, glucose 109, CPK 22, ABG pH 7.4/62.8 She received DuoNeb, admission requested for potential placement. Incidentally patient found to have bilateral subconjunctival hemorrhages. She denied ocular pain and visual changes. Recommended holding aspirin for few days. Patient should take medications for intractable nausea and cough to prevent extreme Valsalva. Consultants: None Procedures: None Anti-infectives: None Discharge Examination: Vitals: 06/23/26 0118 06/23/26 0414 06/23/26 0700 06/23/26 1500 BP: (!) 168/84 (!) 146/80 Pulse: 80 82 Resp: 18 18 Temp: 98 °F (36.7 °C) 98 °F (36.7 °C) TempSrc: Oral Oral SpO2: 95% 97% 97% 96% Weight: Height:
